description: Functions to operate on `duckdb_error_data`, which contains, for example, the error type and message. Please use this interface for all new C API functions, as it supersedes previous error handling approaches.

comment.description:
Creates duckdb_error_data.
Must be destroyed with `duckdb_destroy_error_data`.
Destroys the error data and deallocates its memory.
Returns the duckdb_error_type of the error data.
Returns the error message of the error data. Must not be freed.
Returns whether the error data contains an error or not.
comment.return_value: True, if the error data contains an exception, else false.

comment.description: Returns the number of ExtraInfo key/value pairs attached to the error data.
comment.return_value: The number of ExtraInfo entries, or 0 if error_data is NULL.

comment.description:
Returns the ExtraInfo key at the given index. Must not be freed.
The returned pointer is invalidated by `duckdb_destroy_error_data`.
comment.return_value: The key at the given index, or NULL if the index is out of range or error_data is NULL.

comment.description:
Returns the ExtraInfo value at the given index. Must not be freed.
The returned pointer is invalidated by `duckdb_destroy_error_data`.
comment.return_value: The value at the given index, or NULL if the index is out of range or error_data is NULL.

comment.description:
Returns the ExtraInfo value for the given key (for example "status_code"). Must not be freed.
The returned pointer is invalidated by `duckdb_destroy_error_data`.

param_comments.key: The ExtraInfo key to look up.

comment.return_value: The value for the key, or NULL if the key is absent or error_data/key is NULL.
